title: ledum palustre subsp. groenlandicum
intro: Ledum palustre subsp. groenlandicum, also known as Labrador tea, is a subspecies of the bog rosemary plant, native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Greenland. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Labrador tea include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Labrador tea can be prepared as a tea infusion, typically dried and steeped in hot water, or made into a tincture or salve for topical application.
